+---
+assignees:

+title: JumpToOverlay component (portal, code labels, buffered key matcher)
+---
+## What
+
+Create the visual + interactive overlay that the user sees when Jump-To is invoked. Mirrors `kanban-app/ui/src/components/command-palette.tsx`'s portal/backdrop/modal pattern.
+
+New file: `kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.tsx`.
+
+Behavior:
+
+1. Props: `{ open: boolean; onClose: () => void; }`. The overlay is a controlled component; the open state is owned by `app-shell.tsx` (the next task wires it).
+2. On `open` going `true`:
+   - Read `actions.enumerateVisibleScopes()` from the `SpatialFocusProvider` (the prior task added this API).
+   - Generate codes via `generateSneakCodes(scopes.length)` (the prior task added this util).
+   - Pair scopes with codes 1:1 (in the order returned by enumerate).
+   - Render a portal to `document.body` with:
+     - A semi-transparent backdrop (`fixed inset-0 bg-black/30 z-[overlay-tier]`) — match the z-index tier the command palette uses.
+     - For each `{scope, code}` pair, a small absolutely-positioned label badge at `(rect.left + 4, rect.top + 4)` containing the code (uppercase rendering, lowercase matching). Style: high-contrast pill (e.g., `bg-yellow-300 text-black font-mono px-1 rounded shadow`).
+   - Auto-focus a hidden `<input>` (or attach `keydown` to the overlay's root with `tabIndex={-1}`) so keystrokes are captured by the overlay, not by the underlying app.
+3. Buffered key matching:
+   - Maintain `buffer: string` state.
+   - On each `keydown` for a printable letter `e.key.length === 1 && /[a-zA-Z]/.test(e.key)`:
+     - `next = buffer + e.key.toLowerCase()`
+     - If `next` matches exactly one code → call `spatial_focus(scope.fq)` for that scope, then `onClose()`.
+     - Else if `next` is a prefix of at least one code → `setBuffer(next)`. (Optionally re-render labels with the typed prefix dimmed and remaining letters bold.)
+     - Else (no match) → flash the overlay (set a 150ms `error` state for a red tint) and call `onClose()`.
+   - On `Escape` → `onClose()` without focus change.
+   - On `Backspace` → `setBuffer(buffer.slice(0, -1))`.
+   - On any other key (modifiers, function keys, etc.) → ignored.
+4. Suppression of the global key handler: while the overlay is open, the document-level keydown listener in `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.ts` should NOT fire. Look at how `command-palette.tsx` handles this (probably via `e.stopPropagation()` in its own keydown handler). Match that pattern.
+5. Dismiss on backdrop click — clicking outside any code label calls `onClose()` (mirrors command-palette).
+6. The `spatial_focus` call: use the same Tauri command the rest of the spatial-nav code uses (find via `grep -rn "spatial_focus" kanban-app/ui/src`). If there's a thin React wrapper (e.g., `actions.focus(fq)`), use that.
+
+Style references: `command-palette.tsx` for portal + backdrop layout; `focus-debug-overlay.tsx` for absolutely-positioned labels keyed off rects.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] Component renders nothing when `open === false`.
+- [ ] When open, renders one labeled pill per visible focusable scope at the scope's upper-left corner, above all other UI.
+- [ ] Typing a unique code (single- or multi-letter) calls `spatial_focus(fq)` exactly once and then `onClose()`.
+- [ ] Typing a prefix of multiple codes narrows the candidate set without dispatching focus or closing.
+- [ ] Typing a non-matching letter closes the overlay without changing focus.
+- [ ] `Escape` closes without changing focus.
+- [ ] Backdrop click closes without changing focus.
+- [ ] While open, global keybindings (e.g., vim `j` for `nav.down`) do NOT fire.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] New browser-mode test `kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.browser.test.tsx`:
+  - Mount a tree with a stub `SpatialFocusProvider` whose `enumerateVisibleScopes()` returns 3 scopes with known FQMs and rects.
+  - Render `<JumpToOverlay open onClose={onCloseSpy} />`.
+  - Assert 3 code pills appear at the expected screen positions.
+  - Type the first code's letter(s), assert `spatial_focus` is invoked with the matching FQM and `onCloseSpy` is called.
+  - Type `Escape`, assert `onCloseSpy` called with no `spatial_focus` call.
+  - Type a non-matching letter, assert `onCloseSpy` called with no `spatial_focus` call.
+  - Test the multi-letter case: 30 scopes (forces 2-letter codes), type the first letter of a 2-letter code, assert overlay still open (no dispatch); type the second letter, assert dispatch.
+- [ ] Test command: `cd kanban-app/ui && pnpm test jump-to-overlay` — passes.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` — write the browser test first with stub deps; then implement the component; re-run.
\ No newline at end of file

New file: `kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.tsx`.

Behavior:

1. Props: `{ open: boolean; onClose: () => void; }`. The overlay is a controlled component; the open state is owned by `app-shell.tsx` (the next task wires it).
2. On `open` going `true`:
   - Read `actions.enumerateVisibleScopes()` from the `SpatialFocusProvider` (the prior task added this API).
   - Generate codes via `generateSneakCodes(scopes.length)` (the prior task added this util).
   - Pair scopes with codes 1:1 (in the order returned by enumerate).
   - Render a portal to `document.body` with:
     - A semi-transparent backdrop (`fixed inset-0 bg-black/30 z-[overlay-tier]`) — match the z-index tier the command palette uses.
     - For each `{scope, code}` pair, a small absolutely-positioned label badge at `(rect.left + 4, rect.top + 4)` containing the code (uppercase rendering, lowercase matching). Style: high-contrast pill (e.g., `bg-yellow-300 text-black font-mono px-1 rounded shadow`).
   - Auto-focus a hidden `<input>` (or attach `keydown` to the overlay's root with `tabIndex={-1}`) so keystrokes are captured by the overlay, not by the underlying app.
3. Buffered key matching:
   - Maintain `buffer: string` state.
   - On each `keydown` for a printable letter `e.key.length === 1 && /[a-zA-Z]/.test(e.key)`:
     - `next = buffer + e.key.toLowerCase()`
     - If `next` matches exactly one code → call `spatial_focus(scope.fq)` for that scope, then `onClose()`.
     - Else if `next` is a prefix of at least one code → `setBuffer(next)`. (Optionally re-render labels with the typed prefix dimmed and remaining letters bold.)
     - Else (no match) → flash the overlay (set a 150ms `error` state for a red tint) and call `onClose()`.
   - On `Escape` → `onClose()` without focus change.
   - On `Backspace` → `setBuffer(buffer.slice(0, -1))`.
   - On any other key (modifiers, function keys, etc.) → ignored.
4. Suppression of the global key handler: while the overlay is open, the document-level keydown listener in `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.ts` should NOT fire. Look at how `command-palette.tsx` handles this (probably via `e.stopPropagation()` in its own keydown handler). Match that pattern.

- [ ] Typing a non-matching letter closes the overlay without changing focus.
- [ ] `Escape` closes without changing focus.
- [ ] Backdrop click closes without changing focus.
- [ ] While open, global keybindings (e.g., vim `j` for `nav.down`) do NOT fire.

  - Type `Escape`, assert `onCloseSpy` called with no `spatial_focus` call.
